verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And when the day of Pentecost was fully come, they were all with one accord in one place.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And suddenly there came a sound from heaven.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Dost thou perceive the type?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What is this Pentecost?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The time when the sickle was to be put to the harvest, and the ingathering was made.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: See now the reality, when the time was come to put in the sickle of the word: for here, as the sickle, keen-edged, came the Spirit down.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For hear the words of Christ: “Lift up your eyes,” He said, “and look on the fields, for they are white already to harvest.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And again, “The harvest truly is great, but the laborers are few.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But as the first-fruits of this harvest, He himself took [our nature], and bore it up on high.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Himself first put in the sickle.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Therefore also He calls the Word the Seed.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “When,” it says, “the day of Pentecost was fully come” (Luke viii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 5, 11): that is, when at the Pentecost, while about it, in short.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For it was essential that the present events likewise should take place during the feast, that those who had witnessed the crucifixion of Christ, might also behold these.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And suddenly there came a sound from heaven.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Why did this not come to pass without sensible tokens?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For this reason.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: If even when the fact was such, men said, “They are full of new wine,” what would they not have said, had it been otherwise?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And it is not merely, “there came a sound,” but, “from heaven.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And the suddenness also startled them, and brought all together to the spot.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “As of a rushing mighty wind:” this betokens the exceeding vehemence of the Spirit.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And it filled all the house:” insomuch that those present both believed, and (Edd. τούτους) in this manner were shown to be worthy.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Nor is this all; but what is more awful still, “And there appeared unto them,” it says, “cloven tongues like as of fire.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Observe how it is always, “like as;” and rightly: that you may have no gross sensible notions of the Spirit.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Also, “as it were of a blast:” therefore it was not a wind.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Like as of fire.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For when the Spirit was to be made known to John, then it came upon the head of Christ as in the form of a dove: but now, when a whole multitude was to be converted, it is “like as of fire.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And it sat upon each of them.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This means, that it remained and rested upon them.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For the sitting is significant of settledness and continuance.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Was it upon the twelve that it came?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Not so; but upon the hundred and twenty.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For Peter would not have quoted to no purpose the testimony of the prophet, saying, “And it shall come to pass in the last days, saith the Lord God, I will pour out of My spirit upon all flesh: and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And they were all filled with the Holy Ghost.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For, that the effect may not be to frighten only, therefore is it both “with the Holy Ghost, and with fire.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And began to speak with other tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They receive no other sign, but this first; for it was new to them, and there was no need of any other sign.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And it sat upon each of them,” says the writer.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Observe now, how there is no longer any occasion for that person to grieve, who was not elected as was Matthias, “And they were all filled,” he says; not merely received the grace of the Spirit, but “were filled.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And began to speak with other tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It would not have been said, All, the Apostles also being there present, unless the rest also were partakers.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For were it not so, having above made mention of the Apostles distinctively and by name, he would not now have put them all in one with the rest.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For if, where it was only to be mentioned that they were present, he makes mention of the Apostles apart, much more would he have done so in the case here supposed.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Observe, how when one is continuing in prayer, when one is in charity, then it is that the Spirit draws near.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It put them in mind also of another vision: for as fire did He appear also in the bush.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “As the Spirit gave them utterance, ἀποφθέγγεσθαι (Exod. iii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For the things spoken by them were ἀποφθέγματα, profound utterances.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And,” it says, “there were dwelling at Jerusalem Jews, devout men.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The fact of their dwelling there was a sign of piety: that being of so many nations they should have left country, and home, and relations, and be abiding there.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For, it says, “There were dwelling at Jerusalem Jews, devout men, out of every nation under heaven.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Now when this was noised abroad, the multitude came together, and were confounded. (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Since the event had taken place in a house, of course they came together from without.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The multitude was confounded: was all in commotion.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They marvelled; “Because that every man heard them speak in his own language.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And they were amazed,” it says, “and marvelled, saying one to another, Behold, are not all these which speak Galileans?” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They immediately turned their eyes towards the Apostles.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And how” (it follows) “hear we every man in our own tongue, wherein we were born?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Parthians, and Medes, and Elamites, and the dwellers in Mesopotamia, and in Judea, and Cappadocia, in Pontus, and Asia, Phrygia, and Pamphylia, in Egypt, and in the parts of Libya about Cyrene:” mark how they run from east to west: “and strangers of Rome, Jews and proselytes, Cretes and Arabians, we do hear them speak in our tongues the wonderful works of God.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And, they were all amazed, and were in doubt, saying one to another, What meaneth this?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Others mocking said, These men are full of new wine.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: O the excessive folly!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: O the excessive malignity!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Why it was not even the season for that; for it was Pentecost.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For this was what made it worse: that when those were confessing—men that were Jews, that were Romans, that were proselytes, yea perhaps that had crucified Him—yet these, after so great signs, say, “They are full of new wine!”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But let us look over what has been said from the beginning.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (Recapitulation.)

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And when the day of Pentecost,” etc. “It filled,” he says, “the house.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: That wind πνοὴ was a very pool of water.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This betokened the copiousness, as the fire did the vehemence.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This nowhere happened in the case of the Prophets: for to uninebriated souls such accesses are not attended with much disturbance; but “when they have well drunken,” then indeed it is as here, but with the Prophets it is otherwise.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The roll of a book is given him, and Ezekiel ate what he was about to utter.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And it became in his mouth,” it is said, “as honey for sweetness.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (And again the hand of God touches the tongue of another Prophet; but here it is the Holy Ghost Himself: (Jer. i.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 9) so equal is He in honor with the Father and the Son.)

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And again, on the other hand, Ezekiel calls it “Lamentations, and mourning, and woe.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: To them it might well be in the form of a book; for they still needed similitudes.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Those had to deal with only one nation, and with their own people; but these with the whole world, and with men whom they never knew.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Also Elisha receives the grace through the medium of a mantle (2 Kings xiii.); another by oil, as David (1 Sam. xvi.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 13); and Moses by fire, as we read of him at the bush.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But in the present case it is not so; for the fire itself sat upon them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (But wherefore did the fire not appear so as to fill the house?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Because they would have been terrified.)

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But the story shows, that it is the same here as there.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For you are not to stop at this, that “there appeared unto them cloven tongues,” but note that they were “of fire.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Such a fire as this is able to kindle infinite fuel.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Also, it is well said, Cloven, for they were from one root; that you may learn, that it was an operation sent from the Comforter.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But observe how those men also were first shown to be worthy, and then received the Spirit as worthy.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Thus, for instance, David: what he did among the sheepfolds, the same he did after his victory and trophy; that it might be shown how simple and absolute was his faith.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Again, see Moses despising royalty, and forsaking all, and after forty years taking the lead of the people (Exod. ii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 11); and Samuel occupied there in the temple (1 Sam. iii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 3); Elisha leaving all (1 Kings xix.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 21); Ezekiel again, made manifest by what happened thereafter.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: In this manner, you see, did these also leave all that they had.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They learnt also what human infirmity is, by what they suffered; they learnt that it was not in vain they had done these good works.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (1 Sam. ix. and xi.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Even Saul, having first obtained witness that he was good, thereafter received the Spirit.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But in the same manner as here did none of them receive.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Thus Moses was the greatest of the Prophets, yet he, when others were to receive the Spirit, himself suffered diminution.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But here it is not so; but just as fire kindles as many flames as it will, so here the largeness of the Spirit was shown, in that each one received a fountain of the Spirit; as indeed He Himself had foretold, that those who believe in Him, should have “a well of water springing up into everlasting life.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And good reason that it should be so.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For they did not go forth to argue with Pharaoh, but to wrestle with the devil.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But the wonder is this, that when sent they made no objections; they said not, they were “weak in voice, and of a slow tongue.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For Moses had taught them better.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They said not, they were too young.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Jeremiah had made them wise.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet they had heard of many fearful things, and much greater than were theirs of old time; but they feared to object.—And because they were angels of light, and ministers of things above [“Suddenly there came from heaven,” etc.] To them of old, no one “from heaven” appears, while they as yet follow after a vocation on earth; but now that Man has gone up on high, the Spirit also descends mightily from on high.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “As it were a rushing mighty wind;” making it manifest by this, that nothing shall be able to withstand them, but they shall blow away all adversaries like a heap of dust.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And it filled all the house.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The house also was a symbol of the world.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And it sat upon each of them,” [etc.] and “the multitude came together, and were confounded.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Observe their piety; they pronounce no hasty judgment, but are perplexed: whereas those reckless ones pronounce at once, saying, “These men are full of new wine.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Now it was in order that they might have it in their power, in compliance with the Law, to appear thrice in the year in the Temple, that they dwelt there, these “devout men from all nations.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Observe here, the writer has no intention of flattering them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For he does not say that they pronounced any opinion: but what?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Now when this was noised abroad, the multitude came together, and were confounded.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And well they might be; for they supposed the matter was now coming to an issue against them, on account of the outrage committed against Christ.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Conscience also agitated their souls, the very blood being yet upon their hands, and every thing alarmed them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Behold, are not all these which speak Galileans?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For indeed this was confessed. [“And how hear we”] so much did the sound alarm them. [“Every man in our own tongue,” etc.] for it found the greater part of the world assembled there. [“Parthians and Medes,” etc.] This nerved the Apostles: for, what it was to speak in the Parthian tongue, they knew not but now learnt from what those said.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Here is mention made of nations that were hostile to them, Cretans, Arabians, Egyptians, Persians: and that they would conquer them all was here made manifest.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But as to their being in those countries, they were there in captivity, many of them: or else, the doctrines of the Law had become disseminated [among] the Gentiles in those countries.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: So then the testimony comes from all quarters: from citizens, from foreigners, from proselytes.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “We do hear them speak in our tongues the wonderful works of God.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For it was not only that they spoke (in their tongues), but the things they spoke were wonderful.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Well then might they be in doubt: for never had the like occurred.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Observe the ingenuousness of these men.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They were amazed and were in doubt, saying, “What meaneth this?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But “others mocking said, ‘These men are full of new wine’” (John viii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 48), and therefore mocked.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: O the effrontery!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And what wonder is it?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Since even of the Lord Himself, when casting out devils, they said that He had a devil!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For so it is; wherever impudent assurance exists, it has but one object in view, to speak at all hazards, it cares not what; not that the man should say something real and relevant to the matter of discourse, but that he should speak no matter what. [“They are full of new wine.”]

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Quite a thing of course (is not it?), that men in the midst of such dangers, and dreading the worst, and in such despondency, have the courage to utter such things!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And observe: since this was unlikely; because they would not have been drinking much [at that early hour], they ascribe the whole matter to the quality (of the wine), and say, “They are full” of it.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice, and said unto them.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: In a former place you saw his provident forethought, here you see his manly courage.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For if they were astonished and amazed, was it not as wonderful that he should be able in the midst of such a multitude to find language, he, an unlettered and ignorant man?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: If a man is troubled when he speaks among friends, much more might he be troubled among enemies and bloodthirsty men.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: That they are not drunken, he shows immediately by his very voice, that they are not beside themselves, as the soothsayers: and this too, that they were not constrained by some compulsory force.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What is meant by, “with the eleven?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They expressed themselves through one common voice, and he was the mouth of all.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The eleven stood by as witnesses to what he said.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “He lifted up his voice,” it is said.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: That is, he spoke with great confidence, that they might perceive the grace of the Spirit.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: He who had not endured the questioning of a poor girl, now in the midst of the people, all breathing murder, discourses with such confidence, that this very thing becomes an unquestionable proof of the Resurrection: in the midst of men who could deride and make a joke of such things as these!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What effrontery, think you, must go to that! what impiety, what shamelessness!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For wherever the Holy Spirit is present, He makes men of gold out of men of clay.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Look, I pray you, at Peter now: examine well that timid one, and devoid of understanding; as Christ said, “Are ye also yet without understanding?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 16) the man, who after that marvellous confession was called “Satan.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Consider also the unanimity of the Apostles.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They themselves ceded to him the office of speaking; for it was not necessary that all should speak.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And he lifted up his voice,” and spoke out to them with great boldness.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Such a thing it is to be a spiritual man!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Only let us also bring ourselves into a state meet for the grace from above, and all becomes easy.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For as a man of fire falling into the midst of straw would take no harm, but do it to others: not he could take any harm, but they, in assailing him, destroy themselves.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For the case here was just as if one carrying hay should attack one bearing fire: even so did the Apostles encounter these their adversaries with great boldness.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For what did it harm them, though they were so great a multitude?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Did they not spend all their rage? did they not turn the distress upon themselves?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Of all mankind were ever any so possessed with both rage and terror, as those became possessed?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Were they not in an agony, and were dismayed, and trembled?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For hear what they say, “Do ye wish to bring this man’s blood upon us?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Did they (the Apostles) not fight against poverty and hunger: against ignominy and infamy (for they were accounted deceivers): did they not fight against ridicule and wrath and mockery?—for in their case the contraries met: some laughed at them, others punished them;—were they not made a mark for the wrathful passions, and for the merriment, of whole cities? exposed to factions and conspiracies: to fire, and sword, and wild beasts?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Did not war beset them from every quarter, in ten thousand forms?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And were they any more affected in their minds by all these things, than they would have been at seeing them in a dream or in a picture?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: With bare body they took the field against all the armed, though against them all men had arbitrary power [against them, were]: terrors of rulers, force of arms, in cities and strong walls: without experience, without skill of the tongue, and in the condition of quite ordinary men, matched against juggling conjurors, against impostors, against the whole throng of sophists, of rhetoricians, of philosophers grown mouldy in the Academy and the walks of the Peripatetics, against all these they fought the battle out.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And the man whose occupation had been about lakes, so mastered them, as if it cost him not so much ado as even a contest with dumb fishes: for just as if the opponents he had to outwit were indeed more mute than fishes, so easily did he get the better of them!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And Plato, that talked a deal of nonsense in his day, is silent now, while this man utters his voice everywhere; not among his own countrymen alone, but also among Parthians, and Medes, and Elamites, and in India, and in every part of the earth, and to the extremities of the world.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Where now is Greece, with her big pretentions?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Where the name of Athens?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Where the ravings of the philosophers?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: He of Galilee, he of Bethsaida, he, the uncouth rustic, has overcome them all.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Are you not ashamed—confess it—at the very name of the country of him who has defeated you?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But if you hear his own name too, and learn that he was called Cephas, much more will you hide your faces.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This, this has undone you quite; because you esteem this a reproach, and account glibness of tongue a praise, and want of glibness a disgrace.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: You have not followed the road you ought to have chosen, but leaving the royal road, so easy, so smooth, you have trodden one rough, and steep, and laborious.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And therefore you have not attained unto the kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Why then, it is asked, did not Christ exercise His influence upon Plato, and upon Pythagoras?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Because the mind of Peter was much more philosophical than their minds.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They were in truth children shifted about on all sides by vain glory; but this man was a philosopher, one apt to receive grace.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: If you laugh at these words, it is no wonder; for those aforetime laughed, and said, the men were full of new wine.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But afterwards, when they suffered those bitter calamities, exceeding all others in misery; when they saw their city falling in ruins, and the fire blazing, and the walls hurled to the ground, and those manifold frantic horrors, which no one can find words to express, they did not laugh then.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And you will laugh then, if you have the mind to laugh, when the time of hell is close at hand, when the fire is kindled for your souls.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But why do I speak of the future?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Shall I show you what Peter is, and what Plato, the philosopher?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Let us for the present examine their respective habits, let us see what were the pursuits of each.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The one wasted his time about a set of idle and useless dogmas, and philosophical, as he says, that we may learn that the soul of our philosopher becomes a fly.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Most truly said, a fly! not indeed changed into one, but a fly must have entered upon possession of the soul which dwelt in Plato; for what but a fly is worthy of such ideas!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The man was full of irony, and of jealous feelings against every one else, as if he made it his ambition to introduce nothing useful, either out of his own head or other people’s.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Thus he adopted the metempsychosis from another, and from himself produced the Republic, in which he enacted those laws full of gross turpitude.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Let the women, he says, be in common, and let the virgins go naked, and let them wrestle before the eyes of their lovers, and let there also be common fathers, and let the children begotten be common.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But with us, not nature makes common fathers, but the philosophy of Peter does this; as for that other, it made away with all paternity.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For Plato’s system only tended to make the real father next to unknown, while the false one was introduced.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It plunged the soul into a kind of intoxication and filthy wallowing.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Let all, he says, have intercourse with the women without fear.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The reason why I do not examine the maxims of poets, is, that I may not be charged with ripping up fables.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet I am speaking of fables much more ridiculous than even those.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Where have the poets devised aught so portentous as this?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But (not to enter into the discussion of his other maxims), what say you to these—when he equips the females with arms, and helmets, and greaves, and says that the human race has no occasion to differ from the canine!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Since dogs, he says, the female and the male, do just the same things in common, so let the women do the same works as the men, and let all be turned upside down.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For the devil has always endeavored by their means to show that our race is not more honorable than that of brutes; and, in fact, some have gone to such a pitch of (κενοδοξίας) absurdity, as to affirm that the irrational creatures are endued with reason.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And see in how many various ways he has run riot in the minds of those men!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For whereas their leading men affirmed that our soul passes into flies, and dogs, and brute creatures; those who came after them, being ashamed of this, fell into another kind of turpitude, and invested the brute creatures with all rational science, and made out that the creatures—which were called into existence on our account—are in all respects more honorable than we!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They even attribute to them foreknowledge and piety.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The crow, they say, knows God, and the raven likewise, and they possess gifts of prophecy, and foretell the future; there is justice among them, and polity, and laws.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Perhaps you do not credit the things I am telling you.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And well may you not, nurtured as you have been with sound doctrine; since also, if a man were fed with this fare, he would never believe that there exists a human being who finds pleasure in eating dung.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The dog also among them is jealous, according to Plato.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But when we tell them that these things are fables, and are full of absurdity, ‘You do not enter (ἐνοήσατε) into the higher meaning,’ say they.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: No, we do not enter into this your surpassing nonsense, and may we never do so: for it requires (of course!) an excessively profound mind, to inform me, what all this impiety and confusion would be at.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Are you talking, senseless men, in the language of crows, as the children are wont (in play)?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For you are in very deed children, even as they.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But Peter never thought of saying any of these things: he uttered a voice, like a great light shining out in the dark, a voice which scattered the mist and darkness of the whole world.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Again, his deportment, how gentle it was, how considerate (ἐπιεικὲς); how far above all vainglory; how he looked towards heaven without all self-elation, and this, even when raising up the dead!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But if it had come to be in the power of any one of those senseless people (in mere fantasy of course) to do anything like it, would he not straightway have looked for an altar and a temple to be reared to him, and have wanted to be equal with the gods? since in fact when no such sign is forthcoming, they are forever indulging such fantastic conceits.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And what, pray you, is that Minerva of theirs, and Apollo, and Juno?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They are different kinds of demons among them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And there is a king of theirs, who thinks fit to die for the mere purpose of being accounted equal with the gods.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But not so the men here: no, just the contrary.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Hear how they speak on the occasion of the lame man’s cure.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Ye men of Israel, why look ye so earnestly on us, as though by our own power or holiness we had made him to walk? (ch. iii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: We also are men of like passions with you.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But with those, great is the self-elation, great the bragging; all for the sake of men’s honors, nothing for the pure love of truth and virtue. (φιλοσοφίας ἔνεκεν.)

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For where an action is done for glory, all is worthless.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For though a man possess all, yet if he have not the mastery over this (lust), he forfeits all claim to true philosophy, he is in bondage to the more tyrannical and shameful passion.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Contempt of glory; this it is that is sufficient to teach all that is good, and to banish from the soul every pernicious passion.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: I exhort you therefore to use the most strenuous endeavors to pluck out this passion by the very roots; by no other means can you have good esteem with God, and draw down upon you the benevolent regard of that Eye which never sleepeth.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Wherefore, let us use all earnestness to obtain the enjoyment of that heavenly influence, and thus both escape the trial of present evils, and attain unto the future blessings, through the grace and loving-kindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with Whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, power, honor, now and ever, and to all ages.
